title: spiraea × aemula
intro: Spiraea × aemula, also known as Bridewort or White Meadowsweet, is a hybrid plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its phenolic compounds and flavonoids. Spiraea × aemula has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, colds, and respiratory issues.
